title: Sunday, November 19
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## Sermon Date: November 19, 2017

LITURGICAL CONTEXT
This sermon was preached on the Twenty-Fifth Sunday after Pentecost in the liturgical calendar. According to the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would have been Judges 4:1-7 (Deborah and Barak), Psalm 123, 1 Thessalonians 5:1-11, and Matthew 25:14-30 (the Parable of the Talents). The church was in Ordinary Time, roughly two weeks before the First Sunday of Advent (December 3, 2017).

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS
The Episcopal Church was in a relatively quiet period following General Convention 2018 preparations and discussions around the Presiding Bishop's initiatives on racial reconciliation and immigration. Bishop Robert O'Neill was serving the Diocese of Colorado at this time and would have been engaged with diocesan programs. No major Episcopal leadership transitions or controversies appear to have dominated church news in mid-November 2017.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS
Denver was experiencing late autumn weather with temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit during mid-November 2017. The city's focus was on the approach of Thanksgiving and holiday season preparations. The Capitol Hill area, where Saint John's Cathedral is located, was in a period of continued urban revitalization and development. No major acute local crises appear to have dominated Denver news in the immediate week before November 19.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)
The Trump administration was approximately ten months into the presidency, with ongoing investigations into Russia and the 2016 election dominating national discourse. The Senate was preparing for votes on tax reform legislation, which would become the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act passed later in December. The #MeToo movement was at a cultural peak following the Harvey Weinstein revelations, with weekly new allegations from prominent men in entertainment, media, and politics reshaping national conversation about sexual harassment and assault.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS
The Saudi Arabian government had conducted a major purge of princes and officials on November 4-5, 2017, arresting dozens of high-ranking figures in what was described as a corruption crackdown under Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman. The Rohingya refugee crisis in Myanmar remained an urgent humanitarian concern with hundreds of thousands of displaced people in refugee camps. North Korea continued ballistic missile testing with an ICBM launch test on November 28, 2017 (slightly after this sermon date, but part of the contemporary geopolitical tension).

CULTURAL MOMENT
American culture in mid-November 2017 was marked by deep political polarization and a reckoning with gender-based power dynamics through the #MeToo movement. The holiday season was beginning to emerge as a commercial and cultural focus, with Thanksgiving one week away. Sports culture centered on the NFL's ongoing anthem protests and debate over patriotism, while the Golden State Warriors and Cleveland Cavaliers were preparing for the 2017-18 NBA season. There was a general sense of social upheaval and institutional criticism that would have been felt across American churches and communities.
